title: Fitting quantitative-genetic models

title: Fitting quantitative-genetic models

code: hsquared       R package: friendly modelling interface for applied users HSquared.jl    Julia package: sparse quantitative-genetic engine fit <- hsquared(   y ~ sex + age + animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped),   data = dat,   family = gaussian(),   REML = TRUE ) fit_mv <- hsquared(   cbind(weight, height) ~ sex + animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped),   data = dat,   family = gaussian(),   REML = TRUE,   control = hs_control(     engine = "julia",     engine_control = list(target = "multivariate")   ) )  genetic_covariance(fit_mv) genetic_correlation(fit_mv) heritability(fit_mv) breeding_values(fit_mv) fit_mme <- hsquared(   y ~ sex + age + animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped),   data = dat,   family = gaussian(),   REML = FALSE,   control = hs_control(     engine = "julia",     engine_control = list(       target = "henderson_mme",       variance_components = c(sigma_a2 = 1.2, sigma_e2 = 0.8)     )   ) ) spec <- model_spec(   y ~ sex + age + animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped),   data = dat,   family = gaussian() )  summary(spec) bundle <- hs_data(phenotypes = dat, pedigree = ped) summary(bundle) data_status(bundle)  spec <- model_spec(   y ~ sex + age + animal(1 | id),   data = bundle ) bundle <- hs_data(   phenotypes = dat,   pedigree = ped,   genotypes = geno,   markers = data.frame(     marker = c("m1", "m2"),     chr = c("1", "1"),     pos = c(10, 20)   ) ) bundle <- hs_data(   phenotypes = dat,   expression = expr,   annotation = genes,   annotation_id = "gene_id" ) bundle <- hs_data(   phenotypes = dat,   pedigree = ped,   environment = env,   environment_id = "site" )

"validate" stops parser bridge payload validation without fitting. "julia" exposes advanced opt-bridge explicit target control. backend Planned compute backend. One "auto", "none", "gpu", "cuda", "amdgpu", "metal", "oneapi". precision Planned numeric precision. One "float64" "float32". save Planned fitted-object storage mode. One "minimal", "full", "tiny". engine_control named list engine-specific controls. current experimental Julia bridge recognizes julia_project, initial, iterations, target, variance_components. target = "henderson_mme" supplied-variance validation path requires variance_components named sigma_a2 sigma_e2 values. target = "sparse_reml" experimental, opt-validation path surfaces Julia-owned HSquared.fit_sparse_reml() REML-sparse optimizer; accepts initial (named sigma_a2/sigma_e2) iterations. default, production fitting, variance-component estimation claim public R interface. target = "ai_reml" exposes average-information REML estimator (HSquared.fit_ai_reml()) default engine = "fit" path uses, explicit initial iterations control. validated v0.1 estimator univariate Gaussian animal model; engine = "fit" default ordinary way reach . target = "repeatability" experimental, opt-path repeatability (permanent-environment) model. requires animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped) + permanent(1 | id) formula surfaces Julia-owned HSquared.fit_repeatability_reml() REML-optimizer (three-component initial sigma_a2/sigma_pe2/sigma_e2). REML , default, additive permanent-environment variances identifiable repeated records per individual. target = "two_effect" experimental, opt-path two-effect models. requires animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped) plus second random effect — common_env(1 | group) (IID common-environment effect) maternal_genetic(1 | dam) (maternal genetic effect carrying pedigree relationship) — surfaces Julia-owned HSquared.fit_two_effect_reml() REML-optimizer (three-component initial sigma_a2/sigma_c2/sigma_e2).
